title: Epidendrum biforatum
taxonomy: Epidendrum biforatum is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. This epiphytic orchid belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and is further classified into the tribe Maxillarieae. The species is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. At the genus level, it is placed within the vast and diverse genus Epidendrum, which comprises over 1,000 species of orchids. The classification of Epidendrum biforatum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a member of the orchid family.

intro: Epidendrum biforatum, commonly known as the two-lobed Epidendrum, is a species of orchid found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Epidendrum biforatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
